title: Tamil Kanava Milagu Fry (Squid Pepper Fry)

ingredients.name: squid (kanava), cleaned, cut into 1 cm rings

subs.name: 400 g tiger prawns — cook for only 3 minutes total at step 4
reduce to 1 tsp for medium heat

steps.text: Toss the cleaned squid rings with turmeric, a pinch of salt and half the red chilli powder. Set aside. Coarsely crush the peppercorns and fennel seeds together in a mortar — you want a rough mix, not a fine powder.

Heat gingelly oil in a kadai over high flame until the oil just begins to smoke. Add the curry leaves and green chillies — they will crackle loudly. Add shallots and stir-fry on high for 3 minutes until lightly golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and stir for 30 seconds. Add the remaining red chilli powder and half the pepper-fennel mix. Stir for 15 seconds on high heat.

Add the squid in one layer. Do not stir for 90 seconds — let the squid sear and release its liquid. Then toss everything together on high heat. Stir-fry briskly for 3–4 minutes total. Squid is done when it turns opaque white and the rings curl slightly; do not overcook or it will become rubbery.
Sprinkle the remaining pepper-fennel mix over the squid and toss well for 30 seconds. Squeeze lemon juice over the top, scatter coriander leaves and serve immediately. Squid waits for no one.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: squid → 400 g tiger prawns — cook for only 3 minutes total at step 4; gingelly-oil → coconut oil; black-pepper → reduce to 1 tsp for medium heat; shallot → 1 medium onion, finely sliced
